name: Render Cloud Platform API
what_it_does: Developer-friendly cloud platform (Heroku alternative) with REST API — deploying web services, static sites, cron jobs, private services, and managed PostgreSQL with automatic deploys from Git, zero-downtime deploys, and preview environments via comprehensive management API.
use_cases: Triggering service deployments programmatically via Render Deploy API
Creating and scaling web services, databases, and cron jobs via REST API
Querying service health, logs, and metrics for operational dashboards
Managing preview environments per PR branch via API integration with CI/CD
Automating database provisioning and scaling for staging/production environments
not_for: Complex enterprise infrastructure with custom VPC requirements (use AWS/GCP)
Stateful workloads beyond managed databases (use Kubernetes for complex state)
Very high-traffic applications requiring specialized cloud optimization
best_when: A developer team wants Heroku-like simplicity with modern features (managed PostgreSQL, Redis, preview environments) and a comprehensive REST API for infrastructure automation.
avoid_when: You need complex VPC networking, multi-region active-active databases, or enterprise cloud features beyond simple deployment.

auth.notes: API key from Render account settings. Bearer token auth. Account-scoped access. No OAuth2 or fine-grained scopes. Deploy hook URLs as alternative for simple deploys. Webhook for deploy events.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: static sites unlimited, web services with 750hrs/month (spin-down after 15min inactivity)
pricing.paid_tiers: Individual from $7/service/month; Team from $19/service/month; Enterprise custom
pricing.notes: Generous free tier for static sites. Service-based pricing for paid. US-based San Francisco company. Founded 2018. Managed PostgreSQL (free 90 days, then $7/month). Redis managed. Fast deploy times. Good DX. Used by many startups as Heroku alternative.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits apply; documented in API reference

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Well-documented REST API for services, deploys, databases, environments, and metrics. API key auth. Webhook for deploy events. Deploy hooks as simple trigger alternative. Good error messages. OpenAPI documentation available. Service types: web, private, static site, cron, background worker.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Deploy triggers create new deploys — not idempotent. Service configuration updates idempotent. Environment variable management idempotent. Database reads idempotent.
known_agent_gotchas: Free tier web services spin down after 15 minutes of inactivity (cold starts)
Managed PostgreSQL free tier expires after 90 days — plan for production costs
API key is account-level — no service-scoped keys available
Deploy hooks (URL-based) simpler than API for CI/CD triggering

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS enforced. SOC2 certified. US data residency. Bearer token auth — no scope granularity. Environment variables encrypted at rest. Service isolation between accounts. Deploy hooks with secret token verification. Standard cloud platform security.
